home.title: CASACUSIA · We work for the people, not the ears
home.description: Foundation dedicated to accompanying people with hearing loss and their families. Meetups, podcast, parents network and more.

hero.title: You are not alone.
hero.subtitle: CASACUSIA is an NGO that accompanies people with hearing loss and their families so that no one goes through it alone. We build community, tools, and a network that grows every day.

esencia.title: It's the loneliness.
esencia.body: We work for the people, not the ears. Hearing loss is not cured: it is accepted, lived through, and shared. And when we do it together, everything becomes easier.

aliados.title: Companies and institutions that push us forward
aliados.body: Our network of institutional allies is fundamental to sustain and scale the impact of our free programs.

iniciativas.title: Impact in action
iniciativas.body: Our programs are designed to accompany you at every stage, connect stories, generate tools and foster a more conscious and supportive society.

proximoEncuentro.title: Calendar of Activities
proximoEncuentro.body: Connect with the CASACUSIA community. Check the calendar, join the next in-person or virtual event and sign up directly from here.

banner.title: Want to join the next meetup?
banner.body: Join the CASACUSIA community at our next in-person event. Reserve your spot on Luma.

podcast.title: Our podcast
podcast.body: Conversations, identification and learning about hearing loss. Listen to it with full transcription.

cuatroCaminos.title: There is a place for you
cuatroCaminos.body: It doesn't matter if you are a professional, if you want to donate your time or if you can contribute financially. Every action counts.
cuatroCaminos.donarDesc: Your monthly contribution allows us to sustain the infrastructure and free programs.
cuatroCaminos.voluntariadoDesc: Join one of our commissions and put your talent at the service of the community.
cuatroCaminos.proyectosDesc: If you have a company or brand, we can create social impact alliances.
cuatroCaminos.serviciosDesc: You can contribute your professional services to boost the foundation's reach.

voluntariado.subtitle: We are already {count} volunteers working in {groups} commissions to transform the reality of hearing loss.

nosotros.title: About · What hearing loss is and why CASACUSIA exists
nosotros.description: Hearing loss is an invisible disability affecting 1 in 20 people. Meet the history, mission and values of the CASACUSIA Foundation.

hero.title: We work for the people, not the ears.
hero.subtitle: Did you ever think you were the only person with hearing loss? CASACUSIA was born to show you that many of us go through hearing loss. We seek to transform how each one of us relates to their hearing loss, so we can live better. We seek that no one walks through their hearing loss in solitude.

stats.title: What is hearing loss?
stats.body: Hearing loss is the total or partial decrease in hearing capacity. It can be unilateral or bilateral; mild, moderate, severe or profound. The WHO classifies it as an invisible disability.

world.l: People in the world have some degree of hearing loss.

argentina.l: People in Argentina have hearing loss.

oms.l: WHO projection towards 2050 with disabling hearing loss.

brecha.title: A need that was not being addressed
brecha.p1: Hearing loss is usually addressed from a medical or technical paradigm: audiometries, devices, surgeries. However, many people not only face hearing barriers, but also isolation, shame, lack of information and few spaces of belonging.
brecha.p2: That impacts mental health, socialization, education and employment. Many people go through their process in solitude. CASACUSIA exists to respond to that gap.

mision.label: Mission
mision.title: Transform the relationship of people with their hearing loss, to improve their quality of life.
mision.body: We understand the relationship with hearing loss as the way we see our deck of cards.

vision.label: Vision
vision.title: To be an inspiring movement that accompanies and empowers people with hearing loss, promoting a more conscious and supportive society.

valores.title: Our values
valores.body: The pillars that sustain every project, meeting and decision of the foundation.

cta.title: Join CASACUSIA
cta.body: There are four ways to be part of this movement: donate, volunteer, do projects together or donate your services as a professional.

equipo.title: Team · The people behind CASACUSIA
equipo.description: Meet the team behind the CASACUSIA Foundation: Rama, Valen, Melu, Toto, Nico, Manu, Shari, Lucas, Vero and Judi.
equipo.heading: The people pushing CASACUSIA forward
equipo.subtitle: An interdisciplinary team combining passion, love, ambition for a better world and talent. We seek to listen and be heard. Behind every program there's a person who cares for it.
equipo.ctaVolSub: Beyond the core there are dozens of volunteers organized in commissions.

lucas-adlerstein.bio: Lost his hearing at age 15. Content creator and reference in hearing disability. Founded CASACUSIA to transform how hearing loss is experienced.

rama.bio: Part of the core that sustains CASACUSIA since its inception. Brings strategic vision and experience in the development of social projects.

valen.bio: Coordinates communications at CASACUSIA. Specialist in digital media, public relations and content strategy.

melu.bio: Ensures that teams and projects function with clarity. Her training allows her to manage operations and optimize internal processes.

shari.bio: Leads in-person and virtual meetups. Brings her expertise in group dynamics and community support.

nico.bio: Industrial Engineer (ITBA). Leads the fundraising strategy, developing relationships with donors and strategic allies to ensure the sustainability and growth of CASACUSIA's social impact.

manu.bio: Leads the project for the new website. Specialized in technological development, product design and user experience.

vero.bio: Degree in Communication (UBA) and Ontological Coach. With more than 20 years of experience in regional marketing for major brands, she is co-founder of Chaucha and Hormigas Group. She brings her strategic and human vision to the team.

judi.bio: Accompanies and makes CASACUSIA's mission possible.

historia.title: History · How CASACUSIA started
historia.description: From Lucas' personal experience to the Foundation in January 2025. The story of CASACUSIA.

legal.title: Legal information · CASACUSIA
legal.description: Institutional data of the CASACUSIA Foundation. ARCA approval for income tax deduction.
legal.heading: Legal information
legal.subtitle: We are a Foundation with legal entity status in Argentina.

sumate.title: Join us · 4 ways to be part of CASACUSIA
sumate.description: Donate, volunteer, do projects together or donate services. Four equivalent paths to join CASACUSIA.

hero.title: Four ways to be part.
hero.subtitle: You don't need to donate money. There are four equivalent paths and they all count.

donar.title: Donate · Your contribution transforms lives
donar.description: Support CASACUSIA financially so we can continue providing free programs to the community.
donar.heading: Every contribution sustains this community.
donar.subtitle: Your donation allows us to organize meetups, produce the podcast, sustain the Families Network and accompany hundreds of people every day.

niveles.title: Choose your level of support
niveles.body: Turn the grains into support. You can cancel at any time.

impacto.title: This is what you finance with your contribution
impacto.body: Thanks to the community we can sustain these spaces where we meet and learn.

empresa.label: Companies
empresa.title: Institutional support
empresa.body: CASACUSIA has ARCA approval so that companies can deduct their donations within the current limits. We send you the corresponding tax documentation.
items: You can assign your contribution to a specific program (e.g. Podcast).
Optional joint communication with your sustainability team.
Detailed annual reporting of the impact of your contribution.

cafe.desc: You buy us a coffee a month. Helps sustain the basic infrastructure of the NGO.

toston.desc: A high-impact contribution. You directly help sustain and produce the podcast.

asado.desc: A key contribution to sustain the logistics of our large in-person meetups.

voluntariado.title: Volunteering · CASACUSIA's engine
voluntariado.description: Meet the volunteer team organized by commissions. Today the team is full; you can leave your data for when we reopen.
voluntariado.heading: We are more than what is seen.
voluntariado.subtitle: Behind every meetup, every episode and every message we respond to, there are dozens of volunteers organized by commissions who make all this possible.
voluntariado.eyebrow: Meet those who put their heart into it
voluntariado.equipoCompleto: Today we have a full team
voluntariado.waitlistDesc: We are not bringing in new volunteers at this time. If you want to leave your data for when we reopen calls, write to us telling us about yourself.

proyectosJuntos.title: Projects together · Alliances with companies and institutions
proyectosJuntos.description: Talks, workshops, agreements, research and joint campaigns. There is much to do and fly.
proyectosJuntos.heading: For companies, NGOs, institutions and professionals.
proyectosJuntos.subtitle: One of the obstacles in disability is when it's hard for us to ask. Because asking… expands our field of action. We believe in alliances that combine purpose with serious work. If your organization wants to do something with us, write to us!

charlas.title: Talks
charlas.desc: For companies, universities and schools about hearing loss, inclusion and empathy.

workshops.title: Workshops
workshops.desc: Experiential dynamics to rethink accessibility from day to day.

convenios.title: Agreements
convenios.desc: With universities, professional associations, hospitals and friendly NGOs.

investigacion.title: Research
investigacion.desc: On hearing loss, quality of life and access to rights.

rse.title: CSR with meaning
rse.desc: Projects with measurable impact, not greenwashing.

proyectosJuntos.porqueSub: What we bring to the alliance

porqueItems.title: Experiential legitimacy
porqueItems.desc: Lucas and the team live what we communicate.

porqueItems.title: Active community
porqueItems.desc: Real and engaged audience on IG, TikTok, YouTube and podcast.

porqueItems.title: Territorial network
porqueItems.desc: Presence in CABA, Misiones, Mendoza, Bariloche, Córdoba, La Plata and Madrid.

porqueItems.title: Beloved brand
porqueItems.desc: Warm, human narrative, without technical jargon.

proyectosJuntos.ctaHeading: What do you have in mind?
proyectosJuntos.ctaSub: Write to us telling us about your organization and what kind of alliance you imagine. We reply in a few business days.

donarServicios.title: Donate services · Contribute with your expertise
donarServicios.description: Pro-bono, products, infrastructure or professional hours. Examples of what our allies already donate.
donarServicios.heading: Contribute with what you already know how to do.
donarServicios.subtitle: Not everything is done with money. Professional hours, products, infrastructure or pro-bono: there are many ways to add up.

diseno.title: Graphic design
diseno.desc: Social media pieces, materials for meetups.

pasos: If there is an immediate fit, we coordinate a virtual talk.
If there is no fit now, you stay in our network for a future project.
In any case, we always respond.

contacto.title: Contact · Write to us based on your inquiry
contacto.description: Segmented form by type of inquiry: press, company, volunteering, personal, professional. Also via WhatsApp community.

hero.title: Write to us.
hero.subtitle: Tell us briefly what your inquiry is about and we'll get back to you as soon as we can. You can also join our WhatsApp community to stay close.

contacto.honesty: We're a small team and receive many messages. Sometimes we're slow to reply. If it's urgent, try joining the WhatsApp community or reach us on Instagram first.

form.name: Full name
form.howFound: How did you hear about us? (optional)
form.consent: I agree that CASACUSIA stores this data to reply to my inquiry.
form.success: Thanks! We'll be in touch soon.
form.error: Couldn't send your message. Try again or reach us at our social media.

footer.about: Argentine Foundation committed to improving the quality of life of people with hearing loss through meeting, reflection and human connection.
